summary: QA accepts the Keyword Expansion Agent slice. The implementation provides a reusable typed agents/ module, docs-aligned prompt construction, injected LlmClient safeJsonCompletion usage, deterministic no-secret/no-network fallback, candidate normalization/filtering/count limits, focused test coverage, and README documentation without expanding into forbidden product scope.

acceptance_criteria.reusable_typed_agent: PASS: agents/keyword-expansion-agent.ts exports Zod input/candidate/response schemas, inferred TypeScript types, and generateKeywordCandidates() as the stable public function, re-exported from agents/index.ts.
acceptance_criteria.prompt_and_llm_boundary: PASS: buildKeywordExpansionMessages() matches docs/08 Agent 1 prompt content and generateKeywordCandidates() calls safeJsonCompletion() only through an injected LlmClient.
acceptance_criteria.deterministic_mock_fallback: PASS: fallback generates the default 20 candidates locally without process.env reads, network calls, database writes, paid services, or required keys.
acceptance_criteria.normalization_filtering_limits: PASS: returned candidates are normalized, count-limited, include country/language/intentType/toolTypeGuess/rationale, and filter excludedTopics case-insensitively across keyword plus rationale.
acceptance_criteria.tests: PASS: focused tests cover LLM success, malformed/rejected LLM fallback, excluded topic filtering, deterministic fallback, and count limits.
acceptance_criteria.readme: PASS: README documents the slice, fallback behavior, no-secret/no-network/no-database/no-paid-service policy, npm test, and npm run build.
acceptance_criteria.forbidden_scope: PASS: reviewed agents/ and focused tests contain no SERP analysis, opportunity analysis, scoring, scan orchestration, persistence, API route, UI, auth, cron, deployment, real LLM provider wiring, or paid service setup.

decisions: Accepted the agents/ boundary and public generateKeywordCandidates() contract for this scoped slice.
Accepted injected LlmClient-only safeJsonCompletion usage with no real provider wiring in the agent.
Accepted deterministic mock fallback behavior as sufficient for no-key local development and tests.
Accepted deferring persistence, scan orchestration, API integration, UI integration, and real provider setup to later tasks.

tests_run.result: Passed: 16 tests across 5 suites, including all 5 Keyword Expansion Agent acceptance tests.
Passed: Next.js production build compiled, type-checked, and generated static pages. Non-fatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings remain.
Passed with Git line-ending warnings only.

risks: The deterministic fallback is template-based and may need quality tuning after later SERP/scoring feedback.
The existing non-fatal Windows SWC warning remains visible during builds.
requested_action: Accept M3-KEYWORD-EXPANSION-AGENT-001 and proceed with orchestration.
